notes.note: A Greek proverb (prora kai prymna, 'prow and stern'): the whole of a matter from beginning to end. Settling his accounts was, Cicero says, his entire reason for sending Tiro away.

notes.anchor: the instalment of the Kalends of January
notes.note: Roman debts and rents commonly fell due on the Kalends (first) of a month; the January instalment is the payment Cicero most wants secured.

notes.anchor: Both, I suppose, are bound for the springs
notes.note: The warm baths (aquae) of the bay of Naples or nearby spas; Balbus and Hirtius, both Caesarian insiders, are travelling that way, and Cicero tracks their movements for political news.
